qualifying_org_definition: Religious or community organizations (including schools, hospitals, public service agencies, libraries, nursing homes, churches, and other community organizations); excludes court-ordered community service and unpaid work for family/friends. No pre-approved list, MOU, or provider registration found for the general volunteer lane.
verification_instrument: Written or verbal statement from the volunteer organization (per ND SNAP policy manual §402 and older 43005/430-05-40-50-05). No named state-specific form identified; standard written or verbal org attestation is sufficient.
hours_formula_detail: ND SNAP policy manual §402 explicitly states 'a monthly average of 80 per month or 20 hours per week' — standard federal baseline, no workfare allotment formula.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No ND SNAP policy document reviewed addresses the geographic location of the volunteer organization. The qualifying org definition (schools, hospitals, public service agencies, libraries, nursing homes, churches, other community organizations) contains no in-state or ND-registration requirement. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 accepts any qualifying nonprofit; absent an explicit state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide as of November 1, 2025. ND SNAP policy §403 (Release 25.7, effective 11/1/2025) explicitly lists 'Waived Reservations: None' and 'Waived Counties: None' for the period 7/1/2025 through 10/31/2025. The OBBBA further restricts waiver eligibility to areas with >10% unemployment; no ND county or reservation met that threshold for an approved 2026 waiver.
submission_deadline: By the 10th day of the month following the month in which the work occurred (standard ND SNAP change-reporting rule); specific ABAWD hours deadline not named separately in policy — follows the general reporting-by-10th rule.
notes:
North Dakota policy explicitly allows volunteer/in-kind hours at community or religious organizations to count toward the ABAWD 80-hour/month requirement, verified by a written or verbal statement from the volunteer org. No mandatory prestep (EDP, county assignment, MOU, or provider registration) appears in any policy document reviewed. Confidence is medium rather than high because no named ND-specific volunteer verification form was found, the older 43005 policy manual may not fully reflect post-OBBBA 2025-2026 enforcement details, and the state administers SNAP through county Human Service Zones where local implementation may vary.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial multi-source review to attempt to refute Tier 1 classification. Searched for: ND SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 (PDF link found at hhs.nd.gov but returned 404 on direct fetch); ND SNAP work registration intake and employment plan requirements; approved volunteer site lists; and legal aid/anti-hunger organization guidance on ABAWD verification. Key findings that reinforce Tier 1: (1) ND SNAP policy manual §301 (Work Registration Overview, current as of Release 26.5 effective 6.15.2026) explicitly states 'In North Dakota, E&T participation is voluntary' and that 'signed application and application for review forms are the only verification needed to meet the requirement of registering for work' — no case manager meeting or employment plan required for non-E&T participants. (2) The older 43005 manual §430-05-40-50-05 confirms volunteer work at schools, hospitals, public service agencies, libraries, nursing homes, churches, and other community organizations qualifies with mandatory verification via written or verbal statement from the organization — no approved-site list or MOU required. (3) ND SNAP policy §402 distinguishes the general volunteer lane (independent volunteering, counts toward 80-hour requirement) from formal E&T participation — these are separate pathways that can be combined, with volunteer work usable standalone without any E&T enrollment. (4) NDWORKS and BEST are both described as voluntary programs; non-compliance with a voluntary referral carries no disqualification penalty. (5) Releases 26.1 (1.9.2026) and 26.5 (6.15.2026) updated qualifying/non-qualifying E&T components within §301, but no evidence of new gating or prestep introduced for the general volunteer lane. (6) No approved volunteer site registry, provider registration requirement, or MOU requirement found across four separate search angles. The adversarial pass failed to find any hidden prestep. Tier 1 classification is upheld. Confidence raised to 'high' based on convergent corroboration across: (a) the current SNAP policy manual §301 and §402, (b) the older 43005 manual §430-05-40-50-05, (c) the confirmed voluntary nature of ND E&T programs, and (d) absence of any approved-list or MOU requirement across multiple search angles. The remote_stance field remains 'unknown' as no policy language specifically addresses remote/online volunteer work, but this is not a blocking gap for Tier 1 classification.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: §402 explicitly states '80 per month or 20 hours per week' — confirmed federal_80, no workfare formula. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: policy is silent — the qualifying org definition imposes no location or registration requirement; federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies. (3) Geographic waivers: §403 (Release 25.7, Nov 1 2025) lists 'None' for both waived reservations and counties for 7/1/2025–10/31/2025; OBBBA raises the waiver threshold to >10% unemployment which ND does not meet; fully enforced statewide in 2026. (4) Submission UX: no named ND-specific ABAWD hours form or online portal found; recipients submit to county Human Service Zone offices (paper); general ND SNAP change-reporting rule is by the 10th of the following month. (5) Form fields: no named state form; reasonable baseline fields apply for the written/verbal org attestation.
